assembly_context.name: FourAxisIndustrialArm_Assembly
assembly_context.summary: 创建一个简化的四轴工业机械臂装配体，包含固定底座、回转台、3个通用关节叉（肩/肘/腕）、2个通用连杆（上臂/前臂）、3个销轴和1个U形夹爪。所有零件基于默认尺寸参数化建模，并通过同轴和重合配合进行装配。

global_coordinate_system.y_direction: Left (Side of arm, hinge axis direction)

design_rules: Units: mm for dimensions, m for internal code if required.
Clearance: Pin D19.8 in Hole D20 (0.2mm clearance).

Shim Assumption: To reduce gap between Fork(50mm) and Link(40mm), assume 2.5mm shims or adjust link width to 48mm. Here we assume Link Width is adjusted to 48mm for compactness as per optimization suggestion, or use shims. Let's stick to the prompt's 'Link Width 40mm' but add Shim parts if strictly needed. However, prompt says 'optimize... add shim OR adjust'. I will adjust Link Width to 48mm in modeling instructions to avoid extra part complexity unless explicitly requested as a separate part. Wait, the prompt asks for 'Python modeling code'. I will define the Link with width 48mm to fit the 50mm fork with 1mm clearance each side, which is standard engineering practice for this context, avoiding the need for explicit shim parts in the BOM unless necessary. Actually, let's keep Link at 40mm and add 'Shim' parts? No, the prompt lists specific parts. I will assume the Link width is parameterized to 48mm in the model to fit the 50mm fork tightly, or simply note the gap. Given the 'optimization suggestion', I will set the Link key dimension to 48mm width to match the fork better, or keep 40mm and accept the gap. Let's follow the 'Key Requirements' strictly: Link Width 40mm. The 'Review Comments' suggest adding shims. I will NOT add new parts not in the original list unless critical. instances.name: Upper Arm Link
instances.placement_notes: Connected between Shoulder and Elbow forks

instances.name: Forearm Link
instances.placement_notes: Connected between Elbow and Wrist forks

part.name: Hollow Link
part.shape: Rectangular beam with bearing seats at both ends, hollowed middle
key_dimensions: Length: Variable (300mm for Upper, 250mm for Lower)
Width: 48mm (Adjusted from 40mm to fit 50mm fork with 1mm clearance)

faces.name: end_face_start
faces.purpose: Mates with previous Joint Fork inner face

faces.name: end_face_end
faces.purpose: Mates with next Joint Fork inner face

axes.name: start_hinge_axis_y
axes.direction_relation: along local Y, through start bearing seat

axes.name: end_hinge_axis_y
axes.direction_relation: along local Y, through end bearing seat

assembly_relation_notes: Upper Arm connects Shoulder Fork to Elbow Fork
Forearm connects Elbow Fork to Wrist Fork
Width adjusted to 48mm to minimize lateral play in 50mm forks
standalone_modeling_instructions: Sketch rectangle 48mm x 20mm on YZ plane.
Extrude along X axis for specified length (300mm or 250mm).
At both ends, create cylindrical bearing seats OD40mm, height sufficient to engage fork ears.
Drill D20 holes through bearing seats along Y-axis.
Shell or cut out middle section for weight reduction.
